channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Political alignment on youth social media restrictions reveals deeper societal divides
item.description: The article explores the unexpected political consensus emerging around New Zealand's potential social media ban for minors. Despite traditional left-right divisions, both National and Labour parties ...

item.title: Durban-born Chef Sasha Small's Journey from Insurance to Culinary Excellence
item.description: Sasha Small, a Durban-born chef, made a dramatic career shift after 20 years in the insurance industry to pursue her passion for food. Raised in a spiritually guided family, she emphasizes values like...

item.title: Wildfires Drive Evacuations and Housing Crises in Northwestern Ontario
item.description: Multiple communities across northwestern Ontario are facing mandatory evacuations due to raging wildfires, with heat and air quality warnings remaining active. The Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) Nort...

item.title: Indian Celebrities and Athletes Spotlight at Wimbledon
item.description: The article highlights the growing presence of Indian celebrities, athletes, and influencers at Wimbledon, transforming it into a cultural and social event beyond just tennis. Indian cricket captain S...

item.title: Police investigate killing of American marine biologist in Negros Oriental
item.description: Philippine police have identified the victim's live-in partner and her sibling as key suspects in the fatal shooting of American marine biologist Kent Carpenter inside his home in Sibulan, Negros Orie...

item.title: Kenyan Father Grieves After Losing Wife and Newborn Son Within Two Weeks
item.description: Ismail Bello, a Kenyan father, is experiencing unimaginable grief after losing his wife and newborn son within a fortnight. The tragedy unfolded rapidly: he married his wife in December 2024, and they...

item.title: Minister Vows to Enhance Building Safety After Church Wall Collapse Injures 12 Workers
item.description: A wall under construction at the Nazareth Baptist Church complex in Inanda, Durban, collapsed on Monday afternoon, injuring twelve construction workers, eight of whom required hospitalisation. Public ...

item.title: ICE agents face scrutiny over lack of body cameras during fatal shootings in Maine and Texas
item.description: Homeland Security has pledged to equip ICE agents with body cameras after fatal shootings in Maine and Texas, but officials claim delays are due to government shutdowns. Critics argue the lack of foot...

item.title: Masinde Muliro University Student Dies of Heart Failure, Leaving Academic Community in Mourning
item.description: Precious Chepchirchir, a third-year Bachelor of Education Arts student at Masinde Muliro University, died on 12 July 2026 at South Rift Hospital in Kericho following heart failure. She had been living...

item.title: NRL coach addresses need for reserve grade side after disagreement with Souths Logan coach
item.description: Michael Maguire, coach of the NRL team, has addressed reports suggesting his team may require a reserve grade side following a disagreement with Souths Logan coach Karmichael Hunt. The discussion aros...

item.title: Spain Defeat France To Reach World Cup Final
item.description: Spain stunned France 2-0 in a World Cup semi-final clash, securing their place in the final against either Argentina or England. The European champions showcased tactical brilliance, with Mikel Oyarza...

item.title: CNN Panel Debates Trump's Election Security Speech Claims
item.description: A CNN panel discussion featured a heated exchange between conservative pundit Joe Borelli and Democratic strategist Ashley Allison over former President Donald Trump's upcoming speech on election secu...

item.title: Trump's 'fake news' remark ignites fresh controversy
item.description: The article discusses how former U.S. President Donald Trump's recent accusation that media outlets are spreading 'fake news' has reignited political tensions. It highlights the context of his remarks...

item.title: Zawadi Yamungu's Sama and Basadi Nominations Highlight Indigenous Music's Place in Mainstream
item.description: Zawadi Yamungu, a South African singer, has received her first Sama nomination for Best African Adult Contemporary Album and three Basadi in Music Awards nominations, including Songwriter of the Year....

item.title: USD to Naira Black Market Rate Today: July 15th, 2026
item.description: The article details the current dollar-to-naira exchange rate in the black market (parallel market) as of July 15th, 2026. According to sources at Bureau De Change (BDC), Lagos Parallel Market players...

item.title: Alphamin Resources Reports Record Tin Production and Sales in DRC
item.description: Alphamin Resources, a Canada-JSE-listed company operating in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), has reported significant growth in tin production and sales for the second quarter. The company ach...

item.title: Kenya to Host iGaming AFRIKA Summit 2027 with Global Participation
item.description: The iGaming AFRIKA (iGA) Summit is set to return to Nairobi's Sarit Expo Centre in May 2027, building on the success of its 2026 edition. The inaugural summit attracted over 3,300 delegates from more ...

item.title: Cuba Faces Ongoing Power Crisis Amid Fuel Shortages and U.S. Embargo
item.description: Cuba is experiencing its third nationwide blackout in two weeks, exacerbating an ongoing energy crisis fueled by a U.S. oil embargo and dwindling fuel reserves. The blackout, caused by a generating un...

item.title: Senate impeachment trial of VP Duterte focuses on tax and bank records
item.description: The Senate impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte resumed on July 15, 2026, with no witnesses scheduled to testify. Both the prosecution and defense teams shifted their focus to a critical l...

item.title: Snowbirds Conclude Final Season with Special Stop in Saskatoon
item.description: The Royal Canadian Air Force's Snowbirds aerobatic team, set to retire their classic Tutor jets at the end of their final season, made an unexpected stop in Saskatoon on July 14, 2026. The visit, orga...
